sector_alerts.title: Iran attacks Bahrain and Kuwait with missiles/drones June 28 — US-Iran ceasefire near-collapse, Doha talks June 30
sector_alerts.summary: June 28, 2026: IRGC launched ballistic missiles and drones at US Ali Al Salem Air Base in Kuwait and US Fifth Fleet headquarters in Bahrain, after US struck five Iranian coastal sites. Bahrain: residential building near Manama airport hit; air defenses intercepted projectiles. Kuwait: intercepted two ballistic missiles, no casualties. Iran threatened "complete halt" in talks. Trump warned Iran "will no longer exist" if strikes continue. Hours later: US and Iran agreed to stand down and resume talks in Doha, Qatar on Tuesday June 30. Islamabad Memorandum (Jun 17) survived its closest brush with collapse. Amnesty International June 2026 research characterizes Iran strikes on Bahrain and Saudi Arabia as potential war crimes. Desalination plants supplying drinking water to 73M people across GCC remain at risk.

sector_alerts.title: OpenAI GPT-5.6 launch restricted by US government June 26 — first preemptive government restriction of AI model launch
sector_alerts.summary: June 26, 2026: OpenAI launched GPT-5.6 in three variants (Sol, Terra, Luna) but immediately restricted access to ~20 trusted partners at explicit US government request — the first time in history the US government has preemptively restricted an American AI company's model launch before broad release. Follows Trump executive order (June 2) directing agencies to benchmark advanced models before public release. GPT-5.6 Sol described as most capable OpenAI model for cybersecurity but does not cross OpenAI's "critical" risk threshold. Broad release planned "in coming weeks." EU AI Act August 2 compliance deadline is now 34 days away — all AI labs operating in EU subject to transparency obligations.

sector_alerts.title: Myanmar: UN OHCHR report June 22 documents deepening humanitarian crisis as foreign aid declines — 16.2M in need
sector_alerts.summary: June 22, 2026: UN human rights office (OHCHR) published report documenting how foreign aid decline is compounding suffering in Myanmar from ongoing military attacks. 702+ verified civilian deaths (Aug 2025-Jan 2026); 476 from airstrikes. 16.2M people (1/3 of population) need humanitarian assistance including 5M children. Aid cuts forced program closures, civil society layoffs, closure of women's safe houses. 2026 appeal only 26% funded. Military blockades degrading emergency healthcare. Myanmar was last assessed June 17 — reassessment recommended to incorporate aid-decline dimension.

sector_alerts.title: EU AI Act: August 2, 2026 enforcement deadline is 34 days away — organizations not ready; EU Digital Omnibus voted June 16
sector_alerts.summary: EU AI Act Articles 9–17 and Article 26 (high-risk AI system requirements, transparency obligations, human oversight) become enforceable August 2, 2026 — 34 days from scan date. Research firm survey: most organizations not ready. Penalties up to €15M or 3% of global turnover. Separately: EU Parliament voted June 16, 2026 to adopt Digital Omnibus on AI, proposing amendments that could delay high-risk deadline to Dec 2027, but this has NOT yet been enacted into law — August 2 remains operative deadline. All AI labs with EU operations are subject.

sector_alerts.title: Bolivia state of emergency (June 19-23) — 17 killed, 365 arrested in labor protests; resolved but 90-day restrictions remain
sector_alerts.summary: June 19-23, 2026: Bolivia declared nationwide state of emergency (90 days) restricting mass gatherings and banning road blockades after miner-led protests killed 17 and arrested 365. Workers demanded higher wages, labor reform, and President Paz's resignation, sparked by land-collateral law. Workers' center (COB) reached agreement June 19; emergency declared same day. All blockades lifted June 23. Bolivia was last assessed June 24, likely incorporating these events. Emergency assembly-restriction order remains in effect for 90 days — ongoing civil liberties concern.

entity_reviews.name: Bolivia
entity_reviews.summary: STATE OF EMERGENCY RESOLVED JUN 23 — AFTERMATH (Jun 19-29): Bolivia declared state of emergency Jun 19-20 restricting mass gatherings during nationwide protests by miners, teachers, farmers. 17 killed, 365 arrested. All blockades lifted Jun 23. COB (workers center) reached agreement Jun 19. State of emergency remains in effect for 90 days (restricts assembly). Economic crisis: worst in decades. Bolivia was last assessed Jun 24 — assessment should incorporate state of emergency events.

entity_reviews.name: Mali
entity_reviews.summary: Africa-Sahel batch: 1.5M Malians expected at Crisis+ in lean season. Areas with reduced food rations saw 64% surge in acute hunger. 52.8M food insecure across Sahel lean season (Jun-Aug). No specific new development Jun 29.

entity_reviews.name: Russia

entity_reviews.name: Myanmar
entity_reviews.summary: UN OHCHR REPORT JUN 22 — HUMANITARIAN CRISIS DEEPENS: UN human rights office published report June 22 documenting how foreign aid decline is compounding suffering from ongoing military attacks. 702+ verified civilian deaths documented (Aug 2025-Jan 2026), 476 from airstrikes. 16.2M people (1/3 of population) need humanitarian assistance including 5M children. Aid cuts forced program closures, civil society layoffs, closure of women's safe houses and health centers. 2026 appeal only 26% funded ($234M of $890M). Military blockades degrading emergency healthcare. AI-powered surveillance cited in ongoing civilian targeting.
